summary: Large game-mode decomp/matching PR centered on gm_1BA8 event/game-mode logic, with additional matching work in gm_18A5, All-Star, camera, opening, results, background flash, and sislib. The PR body and human review text were empty, but the diff shows several previously stubbed functions implemented and headers/library prototypes adjusted. decomp.dev reported +572 matched code bytes, +24 data bytes, 3 new matches, 17 unmatched-item improvements, and one small gmopening .sdata2 regression.
key_files: src/melee/gm/gm_1BA8.c — largest change; implemented gm_801BAC9C, gm_801BB758, gm_801BBB64, gm_801BC00C, gm_801BCC9C, gm_801BD028, and gm_801BF128, plus signature/inline-control tweaks

src/melee/gm/gm_1BA8.h — replaced several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with typed signatures such as s32 gm_801BAC9C(GameScene*, s32), s32 gm_801BBB64(void), s32 gm_801BC00C(void), void gm_801BC670(HSD_GObj*), and void gm_801BF128(void)
src/melee/gm/gm_18A5.c — bracket-entry indexing and float literal changes improved fn_8018A514 and fn_80190ABC matching
src/melee/gm/gmallstar.c — All-Star table access and loop rewrites improved gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, and gm_801B5ACC; bot reported gm_801B59AC as a new full match
src/melee/gm/gmcamera.c — noinline wrapper chains, signed temporaries, cleanup loops, and a switch rewrite produced a new full match for gmCamera_801A33BC
src/melee/gm/gmopening.c — implemented gm_801A9DD0 camera/viewport/scissor setup and added static const Vec3 rodata
src/melee/gm/gmresult.c — added fn_80174284_noinline and replaced goto-heavy player-standing checks in fn_801771C0 with a for-loop
src/melee/lb/lbbgflash.c and src/melee/lb/lbbgflash.h — made lbBgFlash_80021A10 public and moved several GXColor values from extern declarations to static definitions
src/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib.c and src/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib.h — changed HSD_SisLib_803A6530 from void to u8* and returned the HSD_SisLib_803A6478 result
what_changed: Implemented multiple previously marked gm_1BA8 functions, especially event/game-mode progression and setup code that uses EventData, gm_804D6900, GmEvent.dat/sqEventInitDataLevelTbl, MatchExitInfo, Player APIs, HSD_Randi, HSD_GObj_SetupProc, and itEvYoshiEgg_Spawn.
Updated gm_1BA8.h so newly decompiled functions have typed prototypes instead of UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS, and corrected gm_801BC670 to take an HSD_GObj* argument.

Adjusted gm_18A5 codegen by changing a loop index type, using indexed BracketEntry writes instead of a separate incremented entry pointer, using pointer-array indexing for BracketSrcEntry lookup, and replacing several extern float labels with literal constants.

Reworked All-Star data access to use base+offset casts for AllstarRoundInfo and gm_803DEBE8_t data, simplified character-copy loops, and updated color/result indexing through byte-level gm_80490940 access.
Reworked camera UI code with noinline wrappers, cleanup loops, and a switch in gmCamera_801A33BC; this depended on HSD_SisLib_803A6530 returning the copied SIS buffer pointer.
Implemented gmopening gm_801A9DD0 as an orthographic camera object setup routine with viewport, scissor, eye/interest vectors, near/far, ortho bounds, and GX link setup.
Simplified gmresult fn_801771C0 from manually unrolled/goto player checks to a 4-slot loop while preserving matching improvement, and added a noinline call wrapper for fn_80174284.
Exposed lbBgFlash_80021A10 through lbbgflash.h so gm_1BA8 can call it directly; lbbgflash also gained static GXColor definitions.
smart_moves: The PR did not just fill local function bodies; it also corrected cross-file APIs and headers when call-site evidence required it, notably HSD_SisLib_803A6530 returning u8* and lbBgFlash_80021A10 becoming public.

The gm_1BA8 work advanced many large functions from 0% to substantial partial matches rather than waiting for perfect struct names; unknown fields stayed as x-offset names where semantics were not proven.
No-inline behavior was recreated with small wrapper/helper chains instead of relying only on broad pragma blocks, making the intended codegen shims easier to search and reuse.
The All-Star changes leaned into relocation/codegen evidence by computing table addresses from a shared base plus offsets instead of assuming direct global indexing would match.
The camera change used the sislib return-value fix to express the real data flow: copy a SIS string and immediately pass the destination pointer to gmCamera_801A2224.
The gmopening implementation placed Vec3 constants as static const data, which matched .rodata according to the bot report, even though the bot also noted a small .sdata2 regression.
decomp_lessons: Small source-shape changes can matter: s32 versus int loop indices, pointer increments versus array indexing, and explicit pointer-array indexing all affected gm_18A5 matching.
When a helper function semantically returns a buffer pointer but the current prototype discards it, updating the library prototype can unlock cleaner and better-matching call sites.
For game-mode table code, direct named globals are not always the best source shape; base+offset casts can better reproduce original data addressing and relocation patterns.
Use typed signatures as soon as call sites are clear. Moving gm_1BA8 declarations away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS made future work safer and more searchable.
No-inline wrappers with explicit suffixes are useful matching artifacts, but keep their names mechanical so they do not imply real game semantics.
Leaving unresolved fields as xB_*, unk_*, or offset casts is acceptable when only layout/codegen evidence exists; this PR did not over-name uncertain EventData/gm_804D6900 fields.
Bot reports should be read for both wins and regressions: this PR gained three new matches and many partial improvements, but gmopening .sdata2 regressed by 4 bytes.
naming_conventions: Address-based names were retained for functions without known semantics, e.g. gm_801BAC9C, gm_801BB758, gm_801BC00C, gm_801BF128, fn_801771C0, and fn_8018A514.
Mechanical codegen helpers used suffixes such as _noinline, _noinline2, _noinline3, and _inner rather than invented semantic names.

Existing semantic types were used where available: GameScene, EventData, MatchExitInfo, PlayerInitData, AllstarRoundInfo, CharacterKind, InternalStageId, HSD_GObj, HSD_Text, HSD_CObj, Vec3, Scissor, and GXColor.
Partially understood locals used short layout-oriented names such as ev, idx, entry, inner, cd, ckind, and costume; unresolved struct fields remained as x0/xB_1/unk_535-style names.
The no-character/sentinel value 0x21 appears repeatedly in event character lists and comparisons; the diff also uses CHKIND_MAX where the type context is clearer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: gm_18A5 fn_8018A514: replaced an incremented BracketEntry* temporary with entries[i] writes and rewrote the source table lookup as ((BracketSrcEntry**)((u8*)entries + 0x3700))[region].
gm_18A5: removed several extern float declarations and used literals such as 0.5f, 415.6922f, 149.0f, 537.0f, 47.0f, and 409.0f to affect constant placement/codegen.

gm_1BA8/gmcamera/gmresult: used wrapper calls like gmCamera_801A25C8_noinline, gmCamera_801A2650_noinline3, gm_801BEFA4_noinline, gm_801BEFC0_noinline, and fn_80174284_noinline to control inlining at specific call sites.
gm_1BA8 gm_801BC670: changed the signature to HSD_GObj* and adjusted PAD_STACK from 0x18 to 0x10, showing stack padding and callback signatures were part of the match.
gmallstar: rewrote direct gm_803DEC4C/gm_803DEBE8 indexing into base+0x31C/base+0x2B8 casts and pointer-difference indexing into gm_80490940.
gmcamera gmCamera_801A33BC: replaced label/goto-style branching with a switch and used the returned HSD_SisLib_803A6530 pointer as an argument to gmCamera_801A2224.
gmresult fn_801771C0: converted four manually expanded player checks into a for-loop over me->player_standings while preserving the result=-1 break behavior.
gmopening gm_801A9DD0: introduced static const Vec3 values to produce rodata and built HSD_CObj setup in source order matching viewport/scissor/ortho operations.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were present in the slice; the PR body was empty.
decomp.dev bot report for GALE01: matched code 68.49% (+0.01%, +572 bytes) and matched data 39.77% (+0.00%, +24 bytes).
Bot reported 3 new matches: gmCamera_801A33BC (+221 bytes, 31.09% to 100%), gmallstar gm_801B59AC (+69 bytes, 72.68% to 100%), and gmopening .rodata (+24 bytes, 0% to 100%).

Bot reported 17 unmatched-item improvements, led by gm_1BA8 gm_801BC00C (+933 bytes to 81.32%), gm_801BB758 (+731 bytes to 94.28%), gm_801BF128 (+645 bytes to 89.67%), gm_801BCC9C (+612 bytes to 95.05%), and gm_801BBB64 (+602 bytes to 72.02%).
Bot reported one regression: gmopening .sdata2 -4 bytes, 50.00% to 40.00%.
